extraction.one_line_summary: BUITRAGO Santiago attacked GEE Derek with 1.5km remaining on Tre Cime di Lavaredo to take second consecutive Giro stage win while ROGLIČ Primož gained 3 seconds on THOMAS Geraint in final 500m.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ Primož switched to mountain bike gearing (44t rear) specifically for Tre Cime, signaling pre-planned late attack capability.
THOMAS Geraint neutralized ROGLIČ inside 1km but couldn't respond to counterattack inside 500m, revealing descending weakness on 20% pitches.
CORT Magnus stayed clear from early break to final 500m, blocking ROGLIČ from bonus seconds and limiting GC gains.
GEE Derek marked PINOT Thibaut for KOM points despite no mathematical jersey chance, wasting stage-winning position for symbolic battle.

rider_signals.BUITRAGO Santiago: positive: caught GEE with 1.5km left on 2300m altitude finish, demonstrating high-altitude native advantage on extreme gradients
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: attacked THOMAS inside final kilometer after bike change to extreme gearing, gaining 3 seconds at 2300m altitude
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: closed initial ROGLIČ attack but cracked to counterattack inside 500m, lost 3 seconds on 20% gradient finish
rider_signals.GEE Derek: negative: caught by BUITRAGO despite early attack, third consecutive podium miss shows inability to close mountain stages alone
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: negative: lost time to both THOMAS and ROGLIČ on Tre Cime, dropped to nearly minute behind with TT remaining
rider_signals.ARENSMAN Thymen: positive: moved into top ten after pacing duties, showing better form than expected late in three-week race
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: positive: survived from early break to final 500m, tactically blocking ROGLIČ from bonus seconds at finish line
